_note: Tool (function) configs for the Callora Vapi assistant. Each tool has its own `server.url` pointing at our shared endpoint with the tenant + function name, so Vapi POSTs a 'tool-calls' message there and we reply with Vapi's expected shape `{ results: [{ toolCallId, result }] }` (handled by lib/adapters/vapi.ts). HOW TO ADD — Dashboard: Vapi -> your Assistant -> Tools -> Add Tool -> Function, paste name/description/parameters and set the Server URL. API: include these in the assistant's `model.tools` array (POST/PATCH /assistant). Set `async:false` so the assistant WAITS for the result (it needs the leadId back). Replace <DOMAIN> with your stable domain (e.g. callora.vercel.app or your custom domain) — NOT the per-deploy hash URL. The optional server.secret is sent as the `x-vapi-secret` header; set VAPI_SECRET to the same value to verify (currently enforced on the webhook route). SEPARATELY, set the ASSISTANT's serverUrl to https://<DOMAIN>/api/webhooks/vapi?tenant=callora for the end-of-call-report (transcript + summary).

function.name: createLead
function.description: Create a new customer lead as soon as you have the caller's name. Call once near the start. Returns a leadId you must reuse in the later tools.

service.description: Service needed, e.g. roof inspection, HVAC repair, plumbing.

function.name: bookAppointment
function.description: Book a service appointment when the customer agrees on a time. Pass the leadId from createLead plus the date and time.

leadId.description: The lead id returned by createLead.

function.name: saveCallSummary
function.description: Save a short summary of the call for the team. Call at the end of the conversation.

leadId.description: The lead id returned by createLead.

summary.description: A one or two sentence summary of what the caller needed.

function.name: requestCallback
function.description: Call the caller's phone and continue the conversation there, when they'd rather talk by phone or ask for a callback. Ask for their phone number first.

phone.description: The caller's phone number to call them on (E.164, e.g. +14255550123).

first_message: Thanks for calling Callora — how can we help with your home today?
